title: NEET centre mix-up: NTA says Nasik candidate himself selected Abu Dhabi option
content: Highlighting the NTA's web-activity records, the test-conducting body said its record indicated that the city was changed through "candidate's own registered login credentials" during the open correction window. The NTA also claimed that the centre was previewed twice from the candidates' credentials. "​Despite that NTA has accorded to aspirant’s request and the change of centre was actioned," the body said. ​​

title: A $60 billion windfall? How US-Iran deal could revive Tehran's oil industry
content: The easing of restrictions to roll back a sanctions regime that was built over more than a decade to isolate Iran economically and curb its nuclear ambitions. The move could allow Tehran to expand sales to a wider pool of buyers after years of relying largely on discounted shipments through opaque trading networks, primarily to independent refiners in China.

title: Microsoft's fastest-growing AI market is the one America fears most
content: Microsoft has built a billion-dollar AI business in China, selling OpenAI's GPT models through Azure to firms like ByteDance, Tencent, Ant Group and Meituan, even as OpenAI and Anthropic refuse to. ByteDance alone spends over $1 billion a year, making it Microsoft's biggest AI customer. OpenAI has privately complained Microsoft isn't doing enough to stop Chinese firms copying its models, a process known as distillation.

title: Death trap in the deep: The silent sinkhole of Bengaluru’s sewage plant
content: Two workers vanished inside a Bengaluru sewage plant, sparking outrage from activists who claim they were illegally hired for hazardous work without safety. The water authority denies manual scavenging, stating the men entered unauthorizedly while attempting gate repairs. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about worker safety and accountability in the city's sanitation sector, with calls for prosecution of responsible officials.

title: First time in 1078-ODI history! Rohit Sharma and Prasidh become first ...
content: Prasidh Krishna and Rohit Sharma etched their names in ODI history as India began their final match against Afghanistan with a bang. Krishna claimed the first three wickets of the innings, all caught by Sharma at first slip. This remarkable bowler-fielder partnership marked a unique first for India in over a thousand ODI matches, setting a dominant tone for the hosts.

title: 'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors. He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: Court warns unchecked police brutality could turn force 'like Nazi Germany'
content: Patna High Court has ordered an FIR against a Buxar SHO for allegedly breaking a man's legs due to his caste. The victim's complaints were ignored by local police. The court rejected the police's claim of an accidental fall, citing X-ray evidence and the victim's fear. The investigation is now transferred to the CID, with the court emphasizing equal application of law and warning against unchecked police brutality.

title: Karnataka youth assault by 'right-wing activists' over lunch with Hindu girlfriend
content: A young man has lodged a complaint with Hubballi rural police, alleging he was assaulted by suspected Right-wing activists for having lunch with his Hindu girlfriend. The group reportedly demanded Aadhaar cards, then assaulted him after he identified as Christian. They also allegedly took his girlfriend's video, made false accusations, and threatened the couple's mother for a ransom.

title: NEET UG re-exam tomorrow: Check complete exam-day guidelines
content: NEET UG Re-Exam 2026 is scheduled for June 21, with over 22 lakh aspirants expected. The National Testing Agency has implemented strict security, including CCTV surveillance and biometric verification. Candidates must bring a new admit card, original ID, and passport-sized photos. The exam duration is extended to 195 minutes, and a new dress code is in effect. Authorities warn against fake news regarding paper leaks.

title: Palantir CEO to Sam Altman and Dario Amodei on why people are unhappy with their companies
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp criticizes OpenAI and Anthropic, stating corporate clients are frustrated with their focus on 'tokenmaxxing' over practical business needs.  He believes the industry must shift from model development to implementation.  Despite disagreements, Karp acknowledges Anthropic's leading position and Palantir's operational ties to the company.  This comes as both OpenAI and Anthropic prepare for potential public offerings.

title: 7 new bullet train corridors approved: Centre steps up high-speed rail expansion
content: India is set to revolutionize its rail network with the approval of seven new bullet train corridors, promising significantly reduced travel times between major cities.  Key routes like Mumbai-Ahmedabad will see journeys slashed to under two hours, while Bengaluru-Chennai will take just over an hour. This ambitious expansion, including the Delhi-Varanasi-Patna corridor, aims to boost connectivity and usher in an era of unprecedented railway development across the nation.

title: China's economy struggles to regain domestic momentum despite export boom: Report
content: Despite strong export growth, China's domestic economy faces significant challenges. Consumer spending saw a decline in May, with retail sales falling for the first time since late 2022. Consumer confidence remains low, and credit demand is weak, indicating reluctance from businesses and households to borrow. The property sector continues to struggle, though some major cities show signs of stabilization. Semiconductor exports, however, are a notable bright spot, surging significantly.

title: 'Yeh Dil Maange More': How Captain Vikram Batra captured Point 5140 on this day 27 years ago
content: Indian Army achieved a pivotal victory at Point 5140, the highest point on the Tololing ridgeline, on June 20, 1999. This strategic capture, led by Captain Sanjeev Singh Jamwal and Captain Vikram Batra, crucial for controlling the Dras sector and the Srinagar-Leh National Highway, was announced with distinct success signals. The triumph paved the way for further Indian advances, marking a turning point in the Kargil War.

title: European mom shares parenting tips she borrowed from Indian mothers
content: A European mother of four, Ksenia Kala, shares how embracing Indian parenting traditions has enriched her family life. From co-sleeping and daily oil massages for babies to thoughtfully choosing Indian names and dressing up in sarees for birthdays, these practices have become cherished parts of her family's story. Kala emphasizes that good parenting ideas can be found anywhere, proving that cultural exchange can lead to beautiful family connections.

title: Brazil president mocks Neymar, calls him 'home office player' - here's why
content: Brazil coach Carlo Ancelotti has confirmed Neymar will be available for the decisive World Cup Group C clash against Scotland after recovering from a calf injury that sidelined him for the first two matches. His return comes as Brazil face uncertainty over Raphinha's hamstring problem, following a 3-0 win over Haiti that boosted their knockout-stage hopes.

title: Government bans 16 fixed-dose drug combinations: Here's the list
content: The Indian government has banned 16 popular drug combinations, deeming them therapeutically unjustified and potentially risky. This decisive action by the Union Health Ministry aims to protect public health and ensure only effective, scientifically validated medicines reach consumers. The prohibited formulations cover pain relief, muscle relaxation, diabetes management, and antibiotic categories, including several widely used antibiotic mixes and certain skincare products.

title: Why is KL Rahul not playing? Shubman makes 3 big changes vs Afghanistan
content: India has made three changes for the final ODI against Afghanistan, with KL Rahul notably absent from the playing XI. Despite already securing the series 2-0, the team aims for a clean sweep. Captain Shubman Gill indicated the side will focus on improving their big-chasing abilities. Afghanistan, opting to bat first, also introduced several new faces to their lineup.

title: Bolivia crisis explained: State of emergency declared after 50 days of protests
content: Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz has declared a state of emergency after nearly 50 days of crippling protests over economic hardship.  The move allows the military to clear widespread road blockades that have led to severe shortages of essential goods.  Demonstrations, fueled by anger over rising costs and subsidy cuts, are demanding the president's resignation, despite recent government concessions.

title: Middle East conflict pushes India to rethink LPG imports as OMCs absorbed price shocks
content: India significantly diversified its LPG imports, tapping the US, Iran, and other nations to reduce Middle East dependence amid regional crisis. While state-run retailers absorbed price hikes, domestic consumption dipped due to tighter supplies and rising costs. The move, though ensuring continuity, highlighted ongoing import reliance and the need for a broader sourcing strategy to mitigate future geopolitical risks and market volatility.

title: Why confident and intelligent people often stay silent in meetings
content: Fear of social isolation often silences dissenting voices, a phenomenon termed the 'Spiral of Silence.'  Individuals, especially in group settings, tend to conform to perceived majority opinions to avoid rejection. This self-censorship, amplified by social media, leads to a false consensus, hindering genuine progress and honest communication in workplaces, classrooms, and friendships.  Breaking this cycle requires fostering environments where diverse perspectives are welcomed and valued.

title: For 25 years, Meena served others’ homes before murder at Delhi doctor’s residence
content: A 45-year-old domestic worker, Meena, was tragically murdered in Southeast Delhi, allegedly by her employer, Dr. Manish Gupta. For 25 years, Meena supported her family, including her son's wife's fertility treatment. Friends recall her mentioning the doctor's increasingly watchful behavior. Her family questions why they weren't informed of his alleged mental health issues, leaving her in a dangerous situation.

title: 20 yrs, 128 witnesses later, Sunetra Pawar’s brother acquitted in 2006 murder case
content: A special court has acquitted eight individuals, including Padamsinh Patil, in the 2006 double murder case of Congress leader Pavanraje Nimbalkar and his driver. The judge dismissed the testimony of the key approver, calling him a liar who changed his statement. The acquittal concludes a nearly two-decade-old case, shifting focus to the political future of the victim's son.

title: Nagpur NEET aspirant gets Abu Dhabi as exam centre
content: A Nagpur NEET aspirant received a shocking hall ticket assigning his exam centre to Abu Dhabi, over 2,500 km away, despite not possessing a passport. The National Testing Agency (NTA) assured rectification, but with the exam imminent, the family faced immense stress arranging travel. This incident follows widespread reports of exam centre changes, even for students who opted to retain their original locations, adding to the anxiety post-paper leak.

title: Twin roadside blasts kill 7 in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
content: Tragedy struck Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on Saturday as twin roadside explosions claimed seven lives in Bannu, a region near Afghanistan. The initial blast targeted a vehicle, followed by a second explosion that occurred as rescuers arrived. Police confirmed both incidents were remotely detonated, with three individuals also sustaining injuries. Authorities have launched a search operation to apprehend those responsible for the deadly attacks.

title: 'He was an obstacle’: Woman, lover strangled 6-yr-old son in UP; dumped body in forest
content: A shocking case has emerged in Meerut where a mother and her lover have been arrested for allegedly strangling her six-year-old son. Police revealed the child was considered an obstacle to their relationship. The lover confessed to the crime and led authorities to the boy's body, which was found dumped in a forest. Further investigation implicated the mother, leading to her arrest.

title: India to acquire 9 retired Jaguar fighter jets from UK to manage ageing fleet
content: The Indian Air Force is acquiring nine retired British Jaguar fighter jets solely for spare parts to bolster its dwindling fleet. Facing a significant squadron deficit, the IAF is relying on these decommissioned aircraft, previously sourced from France and Oman, to maintain its operational readiness. This strategic move ensures a vital supply of components for its deep strike aircraft, extending their service life well into the next decade.

title: 'Insult to Hindu faith': BJP slams Cong for depicting Rahul as Lord Parshuram
content: A political storm has erupted in Varanasi after Congress workers depicted Rahul Gandhi as Lord Parshuram, performing a symbolic milk ablution on his photograph. The BJP has strongly condemned the act, accusing the Congress of disrespecting Hindu faith and using religious symbols for political gain. Congress leaders, however, defended the gesture, questioning the BJP's monopoly on religious figures and asserting Gandhi's distinct identity.

title: Punjabi teen arrested for killing parents and grandmother in US
content: A 19-year-old of Punjabi origin, Gaurav Chopra, has been arrested in Texas for allegedly killing his parents and grandmother. The incident occurred just months after the family visited their native village in India. His brother narrowly escaped the attack, reporting Gaurav fled the scene. Investigators are seeking a motive for the shocking crime.

title: Hardik Pandya ruled out of England ODI series; Kohli to undergo fitness test at CoE
content: Hardik Pandya is out of India's upcoming ODI series due to a quadriceps injury, needing more time to recover for the format. Virat Kohli, recovering from a hamstring issue, is likely to be included subject to a fitness test on June 22. The team selection for the July series is imminent, with both players' availability crucial for future plans.

title: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's 'Maa Inti Bangaram' eyes Rs 10 crore on first day
content: Following a blockbuster theatrical launch, Samantha Ruth Prabhu’s action-packed family drama, 'Maa Inti Bangaram', is keeping the momentum alive on its second day. Spearheaded by a powerful performance from the leading lady, the B.V. Nandini Reddy directorial is proving its mettle at the ticket windows. Following a spectacular double-digit worldwide debut, early real-time indicators point toward strong evening and night show occupancies as the film marches into its critical first weekend.

title: The Mutual Fund retreat: When war panic meets your SIP - what investors should do now
content: Experts warn against panic selling, emphasizing the need to maintain investment discipline. In fact, the simplest advice is this: do nothing. That is the whole answer, and it is harder to follow than it sounds, he says.

title: 'Do you take Eknath seriously?' Sanjay Raut on Shinde's 'yeh toh trailer hai' remark
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut has strongly criticized Maharashtra Deputy CM Eknath Shinde, labeling him a "dishonest leader" who betrayed the party. Raut also drew parallels with West Bengal's Suvendu Adhikari, suggesting both leaders make provocative statements while in power. He warned that public backlash awaits them once their power diminishes, amidst speculation of MPs switching parties.

title: 'Go to auction': Ex-cricketer urges KL Rahul to leave DC as Pant set to return
content: Rishabh Pant is reportedly set for a homecoming to Delhi Capitals, potentially involving a trade with Kuldeep Yadav moving to Lucknow Super Giants. Amidst this, former India cricketer Dodda Ganesh has advised KL Rahul to seek an auction if Pant returns. Rahul has excelled for Delhi Capitals, while Pant's stint with Lucknow was less successful, prompting Ganesh's suggestion for Rahul to consider his options.

title: 'Thought it was terrorist explosion': London train crash survivors 'had broken legs', recalls horror
content: A tragic train collision near Bedford claimed the life of a driver and left over 80 injured, with survivors recounting scenes of panic and devastation. Passengers described being violently thrown, witnessing smoke, and hearing screams as emergency services rushed to the site. The incident involved two East Midlands Railway services heading towards London St Pancras, leading to a complete suspension of services.

title: NSS-Satheesan rift deepens as Sukumaran Nair launches fresh attack on Kerala CM
content: Sukumaran Nair slams Chief Minister for his “refusal” to engage on vital issues, including proposed amendments to the Companies Act.

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE | Heavy rain expected in 10 districts of Tamil Nadu
content: The latest weather bulletin from the Regional Meteorological Centre on Saturday, June 20, said that a significant shift in weather patterns was expected from Sunday, June 21, paving the way for the onset of the Southwest Monsoon.

title: Center must hold meeting with CMs to conduct NEET and other national-level examinations: CWC member Rudra Raju
content: CWC Member Gidugu Rudra Raju asked the Union government to hold a meeting with all the Chief Ministers of the respective States and take their suggestions to ensure foolproof arrangements for the national level examinations to be conducted in 2027

title: Cockroach Janta Party protest LIVE | Protestors shout 'Go Pradhan, Go', while beating plates and spoons
content: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke said the protest demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan will continue

content: Prior to his new appointment, Mr. Sivakumar was serving as the Managing Director of Bengaluru Metropolitan Transport Corporation (BMTC)

title: Namma Metro to start services at 5 a.m. in Bengaluru on International Yoga Day
content: International Yoga Day is observed annually on June 21, with several events, yoga sessions and wellness programmes being organised by government agencies, educational institutions and various organisations across Bengaluru

title: Kerala Health Minister calls for concerted approach to prevent infectious diseases
content: Rapid response cells to be set up at health directorate, district medical offices, and main hospitals for disease prevention

title: Centre bans 16 fixed-dose drug combinations, citing public health concerns
content: FDC drugs are those which contain a combination of two or more active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) in a fixed ratio

content: Chants of “Dharmendra Pradhan must resign” echoed at the venue, as protesters renewed their demand for accountability over issues related to examination management

title: M.K. Stalin questions TVK-led government over changing name of Naan Mudhalvan scheme
content: Reacting to the State government’s decision to rename Naan Mudhalvan as Thiran Tamil Nadu and remove content related to the original scheme from social media platforms, Mr. Stalin said that changing the name would not diminish the gratitude of those who had benefited from it

title: Emotional Uddhav offers to step down as Shiv Sena (UBT) president, Eknath Shinde hints at more defections
content: Mr. Thackeray calls for starting ‘Operation Lotus’ in response to ‘Operation Tiger’ allegedly carried out by the Shiv Sena to lure six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs into its fold and refuted allegations his party was likely to merge with the Congress

title: Kerala Budget: Multi-cropping, tourism push cheers crisis-hit Idukki tea planters
content: Planters and plantation associations have welcomed Revised Budget proposal that State will encourage multi-cropping in hilly areas and introduce a legal amendment to support it

title: Scientists, environmentalists, policy experts raise concerns over Supreme Court-appointed panel on Aravallis
content: The court also appointed Professor Jagdish Krishnaswamy of the Indian Institute for Human Settlements, Bengaluru, and Professor Laxmikant Sharma of the Central University of Haryana as special invitees whom the chairperson may associate with the committee's work, if required

title: Constitution is sacred text that unites all, judge tells law graduates in Shivamogga
content: Describing the legal profession as a noble calling, Justice V. Srishananda said lawyers had a duty to question injustice in society, and fight for the poor and downtrodden.

content: The NEET-UG 2026 ‘paper leak’, and the structural inefficiencies that caused it, explained by The Hindu

title: More than 65,000 candidates to appear for NEET (UG) re-examination in Andhra Pradesh
content: Commissioner of Higher Education Narayana Bharat Gupta said the examination will be conducted in pen-and-paper Optical Mark Recognition mode

title: Search on for mother and child as probe into old-age home in Kerala’s Pathanamthitta deepens
content: According to Leena Subash, chairperson of the Pathanamthitta CWC, three children have so far been rescued from the centre, two of whom have been shifted to their native districts

title: POCSO-accused Bandi Bageerath granted interim bail to appear for BBA examinations
content: Senior officials of the Cyberabad Police confirmed that the interim bail had been granted by the court and that Bageerath would be taken back into custody on June 25 after the completion of his examinations

title: Hundreds join protest rally in Manipur against killing of six Naga civilians
content: The protesters demanded action against those responsible for the killings; they briefly intensified their chants when the convoy of former CM N Biren Singh passed through the North AOC area during the rally

title: BJP claims Rahul Gandhi losing support within Congress, cites Tharoor’s remarks on PM Modi
content: BJP national spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla alleged that Shashi Tharoor’s remarks on PM Modi’s handling of the seafarers issue before the U.S. President contradict Rahul Gandhi’s stand

title: As Mysuru celebrates 12th International Day of Yoga, city seeks to regain its global identity
content: Stakeholders say the number of foreign students visiting Mysuru to learn yoga has declined, as the city faces growing competition from established yoga destinations such as Rishikesh, Haridwar and Pune

title: NEET UG re-exam: Aspirants in Kerala hope for a glitch-free session on June 21
content: About 1.1 lakh students in Kerala registered for the original NEET UG exam held on May 3, as per official estimates

title: Alleged murder of 12-year-old migrant boy in Bengaluru sparks demand for child rights commission probe
content: Noor Bakhtiyar Miyah, a native of Assam, who recently migrated to Bengaluru with his family for work, sought justice for his son, Ariful Islam, who died on June 4

title: Palaniswami objects to amended version of resolution against Mekedatu
content: In a post on social media, Mr. Palaniswami explained that the amendment proposed by Leader of Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin was inserted into the resolution in violation of the rules of the House

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Nationwide mock drill under way; security tightened
content: The nationwide mock drill is aimed at testing preparedness at examination centres and streamlining coordination among various agencies involved in the process

title: Maradu flats case in Kerala: Ex-CPI(M) panchayat president, builders, architect and officials charge-sheeted
content: Former Maradu panchayat president and CPI(M) leader K. A. Devassy, former Maradu panchayat secretary M. Muhammad Ashraf, former junior superintendent P. E. Joseph and former upper division clerk R. Jayaram Naik were arraigned as the accused in all three cases booked by Crime Branch

title: Stop gambling with children's future: Rahul slams government over NEET aspirant exam centre mix-up
content: The family a Nagpur NEET aspirant claimed that he was allocated a school in Abu Dhabi as his exam centre for the re-test on June 21

title: Ex-Minister Padamsinh Patil, 7 others acquitted in 2006 murder of Congress leader Pawanraje Nimbalkar
content: Special CBI court Judge Satyanaryan Navandar said the prosecution failed to prove the chain of conspiracy

title: Gatherings barred, curbs imposed around 16 centres in Dehradun for NEET re-exam
content: To eliminate distractions for students, the use of loudspeakers, political sloganeering, provocative speeches and the distribution of misleading literature have also been banned around the perimeters

title: Pakistani national who inadvertently crossed LoC repatriated
content: A Pakistani national, Asad Khan, a resident of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, was apprehended in Simari village, Kupwara, on June 12 after crossing the Line of Control

title: Kerala HC orders police protection for viral Kumbh Mela woman during Madhya Pradesh visit in abduction case
content: The couple reportedly received a death threat warning that they would be shot, prompting them to approach the Kerala High Court seeking police protection

title: Udhayanidhi urges Speaker to take steps to ensure live telecast of Assembly proceedings
content: He pointed out that while the debate on the resolution opposing the construction of a dam at Mekedatu was telecast live, the telecast was discontinued when the discussion on the Governor’s Address began

title: Schools in Cyberabad may go online one-day-a-week to tackle traffic chaos during monsoon
content: School pick-ups, drop-offs and buses are once again choking key corridors with Cyberabad police exploring weekly-once online classes during the monsoon.

title: Abhishek Banerjee says armed individuals at airport posed security threat; Trinamool claims ‘murder bid’
content: The party’s general secretary was one of the first leaders to come under physical attack after they lost the elections on May 4

title: Police investigate Malayalam actor Lakshmipriya’s defamation complaint against YouTubers
content: Police sources say steps are being taken to remove alleged defamatory contents against the actor from social media platforms and preserve these for investigative purposes

title: What is the Pawanraje Nimbalkar murder case? | Explained
content: The trial for the Pawanraje Nimbalkar murder case has been in progress at the Sessions Court in Mumbai since its commencement in 2011

title: How 33 lakh enumerators are counting India’s population
content: Census 2027 is India’s first fully digital Census and one of the largest data collection exercises in the world. But behind the technology are millions of enumerators, many of them government school teachers, working in extreme heat, facing safety concerns, connectivity issues, app glitches and resistance from residents. This report explores the challenges of conducting a nationwide population count and what it means for India’s future planning and policymaking.

title: ‘Balan’ movie review: A supremely crafted film in which everyone delivers their best
content: The Malayalam film ‘Balan’ is elevated by a strong technical team and fine performances. Director Chidambaram yet again proves his mastery over narrating a unique story

title: BJP busy shopping from other parties but common person can't afford necessities: Congress
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge alleged that households are perishing under the weight of the Modi government's "mismanagement of the economy"

title: Andhra Pradesh aims to become global tourism destination, says Minister Kandula Durgesh
content: Highlighting the achievements of the coalition government in the last two years, he says tourism has been granted industry status and introduced Tourism Policy 2024-29, attracting investments worth nearly ₹20,000 crore

title: Gatherings banned around NEET exam centres in Cyberabad on June 21; nearby photocopy shops to remain shut
content: In a notification issued on Friday (June 19), Cyberabad Police Commissioner M. Ramesh invoked Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) and prohibited the assembly of five or more persons within a 200-metre radius of all NEET examination centres.

title: Kerala Police rope in religious leaders for anti-drug drive ‘Operation Toofan’
content: Home Minister Chennithala declares Samastha Kerala Jem-iyyathul Ulama State president Syed Mohammed Jifri Muthukoya Thangal and Sunni leader Kanthapuram A.P. Aboobacker Musliar as ‘Toofan warriors’

title: Vivek Aggarwal appointed Financial Action Task Force vice-president
content: Currently serving as Secretary in Culture Ministry, Aggarwal’s selection is a significant development for India’s role in global financial governance and efforts to strengthen international cooperation on anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing and illicit financial flows

title: NEET UG re-exam: Nagpur student gets Abu Dhabi centre; NTA to correct ‘allotment error’
content: The NTA has acknowledged the lapse on its part and stated it was a technical glitch and has assured that a revised admit card with Nagpur as centre of choice for the aggrieved student

title: M.P. to form dedicated task force to counter organised forest crime: CM Yadav
content: The Chief Minister also approved formation of a command and control centre for the protection of State’s forests and wildlife

content: With India’s two tallest leaders set to share the stage, Odisha is in line for a host of development projects

content: Argentina’s three shades of blue and Brazil’s canary yellow unsurprisingly dominate the jersey market, followed by those of Portugal, Germany, Spain, England, Belgium, and others

content: Maintaining that the project was being pursued within the legal framework laid down by courts and statutory authorities, the Minister said Karnataka would utilise any opportunity for dialogue to resolve the issue amicably

content: The CPI(M) leader says Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan who had talked at length about resource mobilisation in his podcasts do not have any such measures spelled out in the Budget

content: “It is important to recall that for the past 24 years, my government ensured that pensions were distributed regularly on the 15th of every month, observed as ‘Jan Seva Diwas,” says Naveen Patnaik

content: Donald Trump referred to China’s President Xi Jinping and Narendra Modi as the two greatest leaders he likes the most in terms of power and leverage, and the ability to execute on that

title: Railways Runs Special Trains For NEET Retest, But Return Journey Is Before Exam
content: Lakhs of students are appearing for the NEET (UG) 2026 re-test after the May 3 exam was cancelled amid allegations of a paper leak.

content: According to the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), the meeting will centre on the theme, "Non-traditional security challenges confronting the world today."

title: 'Foreign Degree Is Rs 1 Crore Debt': Is Studying Abroad Still Worth The Price?
content: A weaker rupee and rising tuition fees are forcing Indian families to ask a difficult question: does an overseas degree still deliver the returns?

content: The pool repainting project, which US media have reported cost around $14 million, is part of Trump's drive to put his stamp on Washington.

content: Jyotipriya Mallick is a founding-era worker of the Trinamool Congress and a three-time minister in the erstwhile Mamata Banerjee government.

content: A criminal court in Iran's Qom province ordered 74 stokes for the group, along with a two-year ban on leaving the country and a two-year restriction on participating in artistic activities.

content: The Narendra Modi government is making a concentrated effort to bring back the brightest students and professionals overseas to India.

content: The police stated that the accused "posed as an Additional District Magistrate and preyed on trust," deceiving victims by projecting himself as a senior government officer with close links to top officials.

content: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to the ceasefire on Friday following an escalation in hostilities in Lebanon, according to a US official.

title: Khan Sir Gets Interim Relief, No Arrest Till Next Hearing
content: The court upheld a 'no coercive action' order, directing the police not to take any harsh action against him until the next hearing in connection with the coaching institute firing case.

title: Trump Calls Netanyahu "Warrior PM" As US Intel Warns He May Ruin Iran Deal
content: Trump had earlier fumed at Benjamin Netanyahu over Israel's campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon despite a deal with Tehran to end the war.

title: Rs 20 Note With Death Wish For Aunt Found In Andhra Temple Donation Box
content: The message, written in a requesting tone, read: "Swami, I can no longer bear the harassment from my aunt. Please make sure she dies soon."

title: "I Was Trying To Be Funny, Not The Boss": Trump On Viral G7 Remark
content: The US president drew laughs on Wednesday from his fellow G7 leaders for his quip as he walked into a meeting on the last day of their summit when he said, "I'm the boss."

title: Swiss Miss: Vance cans trip for Iran talks as critics go nuclear over US ditching Israel
content: the preliminary deal, remotely signed by Trump at Versailles after attending the G7 summit, was intended to launch a 60-day sprint towards a comprehensive peace . instead, it has triggered a domestic political firestorm . while the administration insists the MoU is a masterstroke far superior to Barack Obama’s 2015 Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, policy wonks are contesting the claim .
